macro_summary: Stocks shook off Broadcom's guidance miss on Thursday: SPY rose 0.88% to $757.09, the Dow hit a record, and the VIX fell 4.4% to 15.4. Oil eased 1.7% to $93.47 but the Hormuz crisis remains unresolved, while the US10Y edged lower to 4.48%. Today's May Non-Farm Payrolls report lands pre-market—a strong print could reignite rate-hike fears and pressure extended tech names.
commodities_note: WTI slipped to $93.47, still elevated on the 94-day Hormuz disruption. Gold was flat at $4,472. The dollar index held near 99.41.

writeup.entry_block: BLOCKED: +22.3% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster still shows three overbought banks with no NIM relief in sight—US10Y at 4.48% remains well above the 4.20% reset threshold and MAS won't revisit S$NEER until October. OCBC is the relative outperformer with a 15% premium to the cluster median, but DBS and UOB are stuck in mechanical or RSI blocks. Internal divergence is muted; the cluster needs a rate catalyst to become structurally investable again.
singapore.key_development: No new Singapore-specific news; OCBC's RSI block cleared but the extension remains elevated across the board.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster remains overwhelmingly extended—17 of 27 watchlist names are hard-blocked on extreme extension, and the SOXX benchmark is up 25% over the past month. The Broadcom guidance miss provided a healthy reality check, but only AVGO and CRWV have reset enough to reach non-blocked signals. The structural AI capex story is intact, but the entry timing is the problem—the cluster is too hot for fresh capital deployment.
semiconductors.key_development: Broadcom's $16B AI chip forecast miss (vs $17.2B consensus) per Yahoo Finance dragged the sector on Thursday, but only AVGO itself corrected enough to reset its technicals.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is the only cluster showing life for tactical entries—MSFT and GOOG hold reasonable risk/reward setups, while AMZN is testing its 50-day. MSFT's ACCUMULATE was downgraded to WATCH only on technical gate nuance (no near-term catalyst), but the 2.8:1 R:R remains attractive. The cluster is the least extended and offers the most actionable setups as the market rotates from semis.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally intact—tactically the most investable cluster
big_tech.key_development: Google snapped back 4% on Thursday, resetting its oversold condition; Microsoft's new AI models continue to reduce OpenAI concentration risk.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz blockade: 94-day paralysis, fresh US-Iran clashes threaten reopening talks; WTI at $93–95 range.
US forced-labor tariffs: new 10–12.5% tariffs on 60 nations proposed by USTR, EU and Japan targeted—trade war escalation risk.
Fed hawkishness intensifies: Strong ADP data, NFP ahead—rate-hike bets rise; 10Y near 4.49%.
ECB rate hike signals narrow divergence with Fed; Eurozone inflation accelerates.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated energy costs from Hormuz continue to compress consumer spending (Eurozone retail sales fell 0.4%) and raise inflation expectations, keeping the Fed firmly hawkish. The new tariff proposal adds uncertainty for semis and tech supply chains, though ASML and TSM remain resilient on AI demand. Singapore banks remain bid despite NIM compression, while oil-sensitive equities like BE and defense names stay elevated.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Broadcom's AI chip guidance miss triggered a brief sector sell-off, but SPY rebounded to close higher—markets are distinguishing between individual misses and systemic weakness.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain heavy cash stance—17 of 27 watchlist names are hard-blocked on extension. The only near-actionable cluster is BigTech, but entry requires patience on pullbacks.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive—extreme extension regime dominates, only BigTech provides near-actionable setups.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 17 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension—the semis cluster is in a full-blown extension regime. The near-term risk is a sharp correction if NFP or CPI surprises hawkish, unwinding the parabolic moves in memory and AI equipment names.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop.
